# Processing

**Slug:** `processing` ┬Ę **Version:** `2021-03` ┬Ę **Tools:** 64

Configure Engines and Clusters, manage and browse executions of your Tasks, Plans and Promotions.

## plans :: executions

### `processing__executePlan`

```
POST /processing/executions/plans
```

Execute Plan

Allows to run a Plan. 

This endpoint also allows for re-running some plan execution from some failed step : you should then provide execution id and step from which you wish to restart execution. 

_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `PlanExecutable`. Schema: `PlanExecutable`.


### `processing__getAllStepExecutions`

```
GET /processing/executions/plans/{planExecutionId}/steps
```

Get all steps for a plan execution (order by designed execution, only steps, without error handlers)

Get Steps executions status

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `planExecutionId` | path | `string` | yes | Plan execution ID |

### `processing__listAvailablePlansExecutions`

```
GET /processing/executables/plans/executions
```

Get available Plans executions

List all plan executions the user is granted visibility on. 
For each plan execution returns execution and plan identifiers, start and finish timestamps, execution status and counters on done and planned executables within plan. 
This query is paginated and accepts filters as defined in parameters documented above. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `from` | query | `integer` | no | from date time (milliseconds) |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `to` | query | `integer` | no | to date time (milliseconds) |
| `workspaceId` | query | `string` | no | workspace ID the plans should belong to |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `status` | query | `"executing" | "execution_successful" | "execution_failed" | "execution_misfired"` | no | execution status |
| `environmentId` | query | `string` | no | environment ID the plans should belong to |
| `lastDays` | query | `integer` | no | lastDays |


### `processing__listPlanExecutions`

```
GET /processing/executables/plans/{planId}/executions
```

Get Plan executions

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `planId` | path | `string` | yes | plan ID |
| `from` | query | `integer` | no | from date time (milliseconds) |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `to` | query | `integer` | no | to date time (milliseconds) |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `status` | query | `"executing" | "execution_successful" | "execution_failed" | "execution_misfired"` | no | execution status |
| `lastDays` | query | `integer` | no | lastDays |


## promotions :: executions

### `processing__executePromotion`

```
POST /processing/executions/promotions
```

Execute a Promotion

Execute a Promotion from source Environment to target one, according to all the default configuration set on Tasks.  

Detailed rules on promotion executions are available here : https://help.talend.com/r/en-US/Cloud/management-console-user-guide/promotion-rules

In addition to these rules, current execution can be adapted as such : 
* Default scope of Promotion is the whole environment. When narrower scope (Workspace, Task, etc.) please provide advanced option parameter accordingly. 
* Run Profiles and Resources are defaulty updated on target. You can also choose to keep them untouched using parameters. 

Lastly, [promotion simulation and analysis](/apis/orchestration/2021-03/#operation_perform-promotion-analysis) is also available: helpful when check before exec is needed. Automatic-undo of a promotion execution is not provided. 


_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `PromotionExecutableTaskV26`. Schema: `PromotionExecutableTaskV26`.


### `processing__getPromotionExecutionStatus`

```
GET /processing/executions/promotions/{executionId}
```

Get Promotion execution status

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `executionId` | path | `string` | yes | Execution ID |

### `processing__unpairRemoteEngine`

```
DELETE /processing/runtimes/remote-engines/{id}/pairing
```

Unpair Remote Engine

Unpair Remote Engine.

If data service tasks and Route tasks are available in your license, you can maintain OSGi Data Services as deployed while upgrading your Remote Engine, without need to undeploy and redeploy those services. See [Upgrading Remote Engine v2.11.8 onwards for data service Job or Route tasks](https://help.talend.com/r/en-US/Cloud/installation-guide-linux/upgrading-remote-engine-for-esb-tasks) for more details about this specific upgrade process.  

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | path | `string` | yes | remote engine id |
| `keepOsgiDeployments` | query | `boolean` | no | Boolean to indicate if OSGi Routes or data services should be kept as deployed while unpairing - used for Remote Engine upgrade with data service tasks and Route tasks only |
| `keepMicroserviceDeployments` | query | `boolean` | no | Boolean to indicate if microservice Routes or data services should be kept as deployed while unpairing - used for Remote Engine upgrade with data service tasks and Route tasks only |

### `processing__deleteEngineRunProfile`

```
DELETE /processing/runtimes/remote-engines/{engineId}/run-profiles/{runProfileId}
```

Delete engine run profile

Delete engine run profile. 

When in use in some task configuration, deletion is aborted unless you provided some new Run Profile id to use. It that latter case, tasks will be using this other Run Profile and the original Run Profile would be deleted. 

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `engineId` | path | `string` | yes | remote engine id |
| `runProfileId` | path | `string` | yes | run profile id |
| `newRunProfileId` | query | `string` | no | new run profile id to use in tasks |

## tasks

### `processing__listAvailableTasksExecutions`

```
GET /processing/executables/tasks/executions
```

Get Tasks Executions

Get task executions. Available filters are :

* some specific environment as the alternate filter to workspaces
* some specific workspace as the alternate filter to environments
* status of execution
* limit to a number of days in recent history (for example, 1 : yesterday, 5 : past five days)
* datetime period in the past, not exceeding a 60-day range

All those parameters are optional and can be combined at your convenience. Note that the workspace should belong to the environment when both filters (environmentId and workspaceId) are specified in the same call.

When neither the number of days nor a datetime period is provided, the default 60 days time range is applied.

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `from` | query | `integer` | no | Time period filter start date time (milliseconds). With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are: * Executions that started within the specified timef |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | Position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `to` | query | `integer` | no | Time period filter end date time (milliseconds). With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are: * Executions that started within the specified timefra |
| `workspaceId` | query | `string` | no | Workspace filter |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | Number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `status` | query | `"dispatching" | "deploy_failed" | "executing" | "execution_successful" | "execution_rejected" | "execution_failed" | "terminated" | "terminated_timeout" | "terminated_shutdown"` | no | Filter on status of execution: \| Status \| Execution status \| \|-----------------------\|----------------------------------------------------------------------------\| \| dispatching \| EXECUTION_EV |
| `environmentId` | query | `string` | no | Environment filter |
| `lastDays` | query | `integer` | no | Number of days in the past. Must be in range [1, 60]. Default value - 60 |


### `processing__listAvailableTasksExecutionsWithTags`

```
POST /processing/executables/tasks/executions
```

Get available task executions


Get available task executions. Available filters are :

* some specific environment as the alternate filter to workspaces
* some specific workspace as the alternate filter to environments
*  status of execution
* tags associated with tasks : when multiple tags are provided, each of them will be used as a filter, that is to say, the logical AND is applied
* limit to a number of days in recent history (for example, 1 : yesterday, 5 : past five days)
* datetime period in the past, not exceeding a 60-day range

All those parameters are optional and can be combined at your convenience. Note that the workspace should belong to the environment when both filters (environmentId and workspaceId) are specified in the same call.

When neither the number of days nor a datetime period is provided, the default 60 days time range is applied.

_No path/query/header parameters._

**Request body** (`application/json`): `TaskExecutionsFilters`. Schema: `TaskExecutionsFilters`.


### `processing__listTaskExecutions`

```
GET /processing/executables/tasks/{taskId}/executions
```

Get Task Executions

Get task executions

**Parameters**

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `taskId` | path | `string` | yes | task ID |
| `from` | query | `integer` | no | Time period filter start date time (milliseconds). With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are: * Executions that started within the specified timef |
| `offset` | query | `integer` | no | the position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0 |
| `to` | query | `integer` | no | Time period filter end date time (milliseconds). With the `from` and `to` time period filters, the executions to be included in the response are: * Executions that started within the specified timefra |
| `limit` | query | `integer` | no | the number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100 |
| `status` | query | `"dispatching" | "deploy_failed" | "executing" | "execution_successful" | "execution_rejected" | "execution_failed" | "terminated" | "terminated_timeout" | "terminated_shutdown"` | no | Execution status: \| Status \| Execution status \| \|-----------------------\|----------------------------------------------------------------------------\| \| dispatching \| EXECUTION_EVENT_RECEIVED, |
| `lastDays` | query | `integer` | no | Number of days in the past. Must be in range [1, 60]. Default value - 60 |
